title: Localized resumability
intro: This page is server-rendered in one language and ships no component JavaScript. Open the network panel: nothing loads until you interact.
switchHint: These are plain links. The server negotiates the locale, re-renders the whole document, and sets a cookie so the choice sticks. Nothing hydrates; every boundary on the page is correct, including the ones that never load their chunk.
staticHint: This paragraph has no event handlers, so the transform makes no boundary out of it. Its translation was resolved on the server and is plain HTML forever.
staticBody: Ada has {credits, number} credits, updated {updated, date}.
counterHeading: 3 — A translated QRL boundary that upgrades
counterHint: The click handler captures only the named signal, so it extracts into a tiny QRL chunk. The label is a plural of the LIVE count, so on the first write this boundary upgrades and re-translates in the browser — which is why src/i18n.ts calls provideI18nConfig().
serverFnHint: The handler translates with createRequestT over a server-only namespace. That catalog is never in the client graph, and the response is in this request's language.
footer: Rendered on the server. The only script on this page is the resume loader.
